summary: Iter 25 — Reviews Analytics endpoint (GET /api/analytics/reviews) and dashboard section validated end-to-end. Backend: 18/18 pytest passed against live API (auth gate, schema for all 6 periods, default 30d, totals invariant store_landing+vendor_profile==review_clicks, by_store sum matches grand total, share_pct sums to 100±0.5 after seeded events, period=all includes zero-click stores while short periods exclude them, tenant isolation enforced via org_id, regression on /analytics/store-landings, /analytics/detailed, /analytics/top-vendors, /push/analytics). Iter_24 regression: 14/14 still green. Frontend: live-tested on /dashboard (Overview embeds AnalyticsDetailed). reviews-analytics-section renders as SIBLING under data-testid=analytics-detailed (NOT nested in landings or log-eventi). All required content present: title 'Recensioni Google — Analitico click', period label updates ('Ultimi 30 giorni' → 'Oggi' → 'Ultimi 7 giorni'), 3 KPI tiles (reviews-total-clicks=0, Da Landing Negozio=0, Da Profilo Vendor=0), empty-state message 'Nessun click recensione registrato in questo periodo.' shown. Period-select integration verified — switching period re-fetches and updates the reviews section in lockstep with the rest of the dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: analytics.py:870 — sort key uses `r['name'].lower()` which assumes name is always a non-None string. Stores with NULL name would crash with AttributeError. Defensive: use `(r['name'] or '').lower()`. Low priority — store creation forces name field, but worth a fallback.

analytics.py:879-882 — the timeline pipeline embeds a conditional dict via inline `if` that evaluates to `{'event_type': '__never__'}` when vendor_ids is empty. This is a working sentinel but the inline ternary inside a $or list is fragile (Python evaluates it at pipeline construction). Cleaner: build the $or list with two appends conditionally before the aggregate call. Functional today (`vendor_ids` empty case is rare for tenants with stores).

analytics.py:893 — bucket key uses %Y-%m-%d but the day grouping is done client-side after pulling every timestamp string. For large tenants (>>5000 review_click rows over 30 days) this loses Mongo's aggregation efficiency. Future: push the $dateFromString + $dateToString grouping into the aggregation pipeline (same pattern used at line 1019 for daily-counter). Not a regression — endpoint is fast under current scale.

AnalyticsDetailed.js:411 — ReviewsAnalyticsSection is gated on `reviewsData` truthy (always rendered after first fetch). Good — matches the requirement that the section ALWAYS appears in admin mode even with totals=0, with the empty-state message.

AnalyticsDetailed.js:412 — passes `period` prop but ReviewsAnalyticsSection re-derives label from a local map. Mirrors the existing StoreLandingsSection pattern. Acceptable, but could be hoisted to a shared util `periodToLabel(period)` to avoid drift if a new period label is added in the future.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_analytics_reviews.py (NEW — 18 tests covering auth, schema for all 6 periods, default period, totals invariant, by_store sum invariant, share_pct sums~100 with seeded events, period=all includes empty stores while short periods exclude them, tenant isolation, regression on 4 sibling analytics endpoints)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (section presence, content, KPIs, empty state, DOM separation, period selector integration all verified live)

test_credentials: admin@example.com / admin123 (org admin) — verified via /api/auth/login on REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL
seed_data_creation: Test test_share_pct_sums_100 seeds 3 store_landing_review_click events via public POST /api/analytics for the first store in admin's org. These persist in db.analytics but are scoped to admin's tenant and do not leak across orgs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GET /api/analytics/reviews is now the third analytics-domain endpoint (alongside /analytics/store-landings and /analytics/detailed). The unified `_period_to_dates` helper at analytics.py:172 now supports six period labels: today, yesterday, 7d, 30d (default), month, all. period='all' returns a 1970-epoch start so $gte still hits the timestamp index — usable on huge collections. Frontend integration point: AnalyticsDetailed.js:148-153 fetches /analytics/reviews alongside /analytics/store-landings inside the same admin-only block in fetchData; both re-run whenever `period` changes. Section data-testid is `reviews-analytics-section` and the headline KPI is `reviews-total-clicks`. Per-row testid `reviews-store-row` will render only when totals > 0 or period='all'. Existing test_analytics_reset.py (iter_24) still 14/14 green — the new endpoint did not break the reset/audit flows. ENV: tests read BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with fallback to /app/frontend/.env parse.
rca of the issue: Not a bug fix — feature addition (Reviews Analytics dashboard endpoint + UI section). Requirements coverage: (1) auth gate via Depends(get_current_user) → anon 401 ✅; (2) schema {period, totals, by_store, timeline} stable across all 6 period values ✅; (3) totals invariant store_landing+vendor_profile==review_clicks holds (asserted on all 6 periods) ✅; (4) share_pct sums ~100 (asserted with seeded events) ✅; (5) period=all includes 0-click stores, others exclude them ✅; (6) two-source aggregation (store_landing_review_click via store_id + review_click via vendor_id→vendor.store_id) joined correctly per code review of analytics.py:813-839 ✅; (7) tenant isolation: store_filter scoped to organization_id at line 788-790, vendor join restricted to store_ids of org at line 807-809 — admin only sees own org stores ✅; (8) regression: /analytics/store-landings + /analytics/detailed + /analytics/top-vendors + /push/analytics all 200 ✅; (9) iter_24 reset/audit 14/14 green ✅. Frontend: section appears as a sibling SECTION under <div data-testid='analytics-detailed'> (NOT nested in landings or log eventi card) ✅; period selector triggers re-fetch and label updates ✅; empty-state message renders with 3 zeroed KPIs when totals.review_clicks=0 (current dashboard state for admin@example.com — no review clicks in DB) ✅.
